arts.ttl: Crystal Palace vs Arsenal: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Crystal Palace host Arsenal in the Premier League on Saturday. Preview includes team news, predicted lineups, how to watch on TV and more.

arts.ttl: Arsenal send first Dani Olmo transfer 'offer' as Mikel Arteta seeks to avoid nightmare scenario
arts.sum: It is up until then that players accumulating five yellow cards receive an automatic one-match ban. According to El Nacional, the Gunners have already made their first move for Barcelona midfielder Dani Olmo.

arts.ttl: Arsenal news: Mikel Arteta told what he needs to do next amid Declan Rice transfer claim
arts.sum: Arsenal hope to have two players back from injury for Saturday evening's Premier League clash away to Crystal Palace as Mikel Arteta celebrates five years in charge

arts.ttl: Ian Wright leaps to Marcus Rashford Man Utd defence as Arsenal transfer verdict sent
arts.sum: Ian Wright has stood with Marcus Rashford against the Manchester United forward's critics by saying he can still perform at a world-class level, amid increasing speculation that he could join Arsenal. "For me, personally, I think I'm ready for a new challenge and the next steps," Rashford said, adding: "When I leave it's going to be 'no hard feelings'.

arts.ttl: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal in talks to sign Gyökeres
arts.sum: Arsenal are looking at Sporting CP's Viktor Gyökeres and RB Leipzig's Benjamin Šeško, while MLS and Saudi Arabian clubs are interested in Manchester City's Kevin De Bruyne . - Arsenal are in negotiations to sign Sporting CP striker Viktor Gyökeres but RB Leipzig's Benjamin Šeško remains the Gunners' priority, reports Football Transfers .

arts.ttl: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta's admission on managing Premier League rival speaks volumes
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta - who is preparing Arsenal to face Crystal Palace on Saturday - said it was “at the moment impossible” to envision himself managing a different Premier League side

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta admits he was 'TERRIFIED' when he took the Arsenal manager's job... as the Spanish boss celebrates five years in charge at the Emirates
arts.sum: Arteta landed his first managerial job in December 2019 when the Gunners came calling after he had previously worked under Pep Guardiola at Manchester City.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta admits feeling ‘terrified’ on first day as Arsenal manager
arts.sum: Coach says he was determined to change culture of club Declan Rice expected to return against Crystal Palace Mikel Arteta has admitted he felt “terrified” on his first day as Arsenal manager but was determined to change the culture of the club to enable them to challenge for trophies. Arteta celebrated the fifth anniversary of being thrust into his first managerial role on Friday and was in reflective mood before Saturday’s trip to Crystal Palace. The Spaniard was only 37 when he was appointed at the Emirates in December 2019 and is now the Premier League’s third longest-serving manager behind Pep Guardiola – whom Arteta worked for as an assistant at Manchester City – and Brentford’s Thomas Frank. But he acknowledged that while it had been “a dream come true” to be named Arsenal manager, the prospect of taking over a club that were languishing in 10th place had proven a challenge given his lack of experience. arts.ttl: Arteta was 'terrified' at start of Arsenal tenure
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta says he was initially "terrified" when taking over as manager of Arsenal five years ago.
